mission.title: Install and Configure Flowise
mission.description: Flowise is an AI platform that allows users to build AI agents visually. It is ideal for developers looking to create and manage AI workflows without deep technical expertise. Key trade-offs include the need for Node.js and Docker for installation and operation.

steps.title: Install the Flowise CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the Flowise command-line interface globally on your machine.

```bash

npm install -g flowise@3.0.13

```

> Installs the specific version of Flowise globally.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This command creates a new Kubernetes cluster with the specified version and node count.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create flowise-cluster --cluster-version=1.30 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> Ensure you have gcloud CLI installed and authenticated.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This command configures your local kubeconfig to connect to the newly created cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials flowise-cluster --region=us-central1

```

> This sets up your kubeconfig for the new cluster.

steps.title: Deploy Flowise on Kubernetes
steps.description:
This command deploys Flowise using a Helm chart.

```bash

helm repo add flowise https://flowiseai.github.io/Flowise

helm install flowise flowise/flowise --version 3.0.13 --namespace flowise --create-namespace

```

> Adds the Flowise Helm repository.
> Installs Flowise in the specified namespace.

steps.title: Verify the Deployment
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the Flowise pods to ensure they are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n flowise

```

> Look for the status of the Flowise pods to be 'Running'.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step involves setting up autoscaling and monitoring for your Flowise deployment.

```bash

kubectl autoscale deployment flowise --cpu-percent=50 --min=1 --max=5 -n flowise

kubectl apply -f monitoring-config.yaml

```

> Sets up horizontal pod autoscaling.
> Apply your monitoring configuration.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Flowise
uninstall.description:
This command removes Flowise from your Kubernetes cluster. Be aware that this will delete all associated resources.

```bash

helm uninstall flowise --namespace flowise

gcloud container clusters delete flowise-cluster --region=us-central1 --quiet

```

> This removes the Flowise deployment.
> This deletes the entire cluster and all associated resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Flowise
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your data. This step involves draining the nodes, upgrading the deployment, and verifying the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl drain <node-name> --ignore-daemonsets

helm upgrade flowise flowise/flowise --version 3.0.13 --namespace flowise

kubectl get pods -n flowise

```

> Replace <node-name> with your actual node name.
> Upgrades Flowise to the specified version.
> Verify that the pods are running after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified image may not exist or there may be network issues.

**Fix:**
Check the image name and ensure your cluster has internet access.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The application may be failing to start due to misconfiguration.

**Fix:**
Check the logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n flowise' for more details.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster may not have enough resources allocated.

**Fix:**
Increase the node size or number of nodes in your cluster.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Connection refused'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Flowise service may not be running or exposed correctly.

**Fix:**
Ensure the service is running with 'kubectl get services -n flowise' and check the service type.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new AI models, improved UI components, and enhanced performance metrics.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from 2.x, ensure to migrate your configurations to the new format.

resolution.summary: You should see the Flowise UI accessible at http://<your-cluster-ip>:3000 with all services running.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before proceeding.
